article.title: Sea's View on the Future of Agentic Software Development with Codex

harvestedTerms.summary: An interview with Sea Limited's Co-Founder David Chen on deploying OpenAI's Codex across their engineering organisation. The piece covers AI-assisted coding in large microservices architectures, CI/CD integration of AI agents, the evolution of the developer role toward 'system orchestrator,' and Sea's Codex Hackathon Series across Southeast Asia. Primarily an enterprise adoption story with some substantive framing around how agentic workflows restructure engineering teams.

article.title: An Interview with Ben Thompson at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference

harvestedTerms.summary: A paywalled Stratechery interview with Ben Thompson at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The excerpt reveals the topics covered — compute shortage implications for Aggregation Theory, consumer AI, and the 'agentic future' — but the full content is subscriber-only. The visible material is primarily navigational/promotional boilerplate with minimal substantive terminology.

A Hugging Face Blog technical deep-dive into asynchronous continuous batching for LLM inference, explaining how to overlap CPU and GPU work using CUDA streams and events to eliminate idle GPU time — achieving a ~24% throughput gain with no model changes. Covers synchronous vs. asynchronous batching, CUDA stream semantics, race conditions, and carry-over patterns.

mustReadFlagged.reason: A clear, first-principles engineering explainer on a real and underappreciated performance bottleneck in LLM serving — the CPU-GPU synchronization gap in continuous batching. Rare in that it builds intuition from scratch (CUDA streams, events, race conditions) rather than summarising results, making it useful for anyone reasoning about inference infrastructure rather than just following product announcements.

article.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle

harvestedTerms.summary: OpenAI reorganization article: Greg Brockman officially takes product leadership, with a stated strategy to merge ChatGPT and Codex into a 'single agentic platform.' The piece is primarily a corporate org-chart news story with limited terminological novelty.

Simon Willison's brief post announcing a QR code generator tool he built with Claude's assistance, tagged under vibe-coding. Minimal substantive content beyond the tool description and a few related article titles.

article.title: PwC is deploying Claude to build technology, execute deals, and reinvent enterprise functions for clients

harvestedTerms.summary: Anthropic and PwC announce an expanded strategic alliance deploying Claude, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork across PwC's global workforce of hundreds of thousands. The partnership centers on three focus areas: agentic technology build, AI-native deal-making, and enterprise function reinvention. Notable elements include a joint Center of Excellence, certification of 30,000 professionals, a new 'Office of the CFO' business group, and live production deployments across insurance, healthcare, sports, and cybersecurity with reported 70% delivery improvements.
